400.description: Cannot process the request because it is malformed or incorrect. Review the request URL, headers, and body for errors.
400.resolution: Check the API documentation for the correct request format. Validate JSON payload and query parameters.

401.description: Required authentication information is either missing or not valid for the resource. Check that the access token is valid and has not expired.
401.resolution: Acquire a new access token. Ensure the token has the required scopes/permissions for the API endpoint.

403.description: Access is denied to the requested resource. The user or app might not have enough permission.
403.resolution: Check that the application has the required API permissions and that admin consent has been granted if needed.

404.description: The requested resource does not exist. The resource may have been deleted or the URL may be incorrect.
